jokes.text: Who wouldn't like a bottle of real French perfume all the way from gay Paree? Four bucks, plus tax.
jokes.setup: Bart describing expensive French perfume from Paris
jokes.punchline: Reveals it only cost $4 plus tax
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and character-appropriate cheapness from Bart

jokes.text: Oh, big deal. Dry macaroni, spray paint and glue. Whoopie.
jokes.setup: Lisa's handmade birthday card
jokes.punchline: Bart's dismissive description of the craft materials
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Bart's sarcastic dismissal of his sister's efforts

jokes.text: Litha, my 'ongue' is 'uck' in the 'eaters.' My 'ongue'!
jokes.setup: Bart gets first dibs on licking beaters
jokes.punchline: Tongue gets stuck, can't pronounce letters that require tongue
jokes.explanation: Clever physical comedy with linguistic payoff - the speech impediment matches the injury

jokes.text: My birthday? It's my birthday? - No!
jokes.setup: Family surprises Marge with birthday breakfast
jokes.punchline: Homer genuinely doesn't know it's his wife's birthday
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - his self-centeredness and obliviousness on full display

jokes.text: But it's a surprise. You know, it's such a beautiful morning, I think I'll take a little stroll around the block.
jokes.setup: Homer needs to come up with a gift explanation
jokes.punchline: Immediately flees to go shopping
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer panic response - transparent excuse to escape

jokes.text: I think he forgot, Mom. - Mm-hmm.
jokes.setup: Homer's obvious panic exit
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact observation and Marge's resigned agreement
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - Lisa's intelligence contrasts with Homer's transparency

jokes.text: Good morning, consumers. The Springfield Mall is now open for your spending needs.
jokes.setup: Standard mall opening announcement
jokes.punchline: Bluntly calling customers 'consumers' and focusing on 'spending needs'
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical observation about consumer culture disguised as routine announcement

jokes.text: Too salty. Um... nah. Hmm? Nah, too corny. Too exciting.
jokes.setup: Homer shopping for last-minute gift
jokes.punchline: Rejecting a gift for being 'too exciting'
jokes.explanation: Homer's backwards gift logic - avoiding anything his wife might actually enjoy

jokes.text: And when he surprised you with the Connie Chung calendar?
jokes.setup: Examples of Homer's selfish gifts
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific and inappropriate gift choice
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully random and specific - perfectly captures Homer's oblivious gift-giving

jokes.text: And the kids. - Fair enough. - And my sisters. - Doh!
jokes.setup: Homer excited about romantic dinner for two
jokes.punchline: Progressive revelation that it includes kids, then sisters, culminating in 'D'oh!'
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalating structure with iconic Homer catchphrase as payoff

jokes.text: Havin' my baby / What a lovely way to say how much you love me / Nearer my God to thee
jokes.setup: Singing Sirloin waiters performing
jokes.punchline: Bizarre medley jumping from love song to hymn
jokes.explanation: Absurd song selection creates uncomfortable restaurant atmosphere

jokes.text: 34 years old. Time enough to start over with a new man. Someone who eats with his mouth shut.
jokes.setup: Marge's birthday celebration
jokes.punchline: Patty immediately calculating divorce timeline
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Patty's hostility toward Homer and marriage cynicism

jokes.text: Do you know the Heimlich Maneuver? - No. - Good.
jokes.setup: Homer choking on his food
jokes.punchline: Patty relieved that Selma can't save him
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - economical structure with perfect character motivation

jokes.text: What the hell are you talking about? There's gallons of it! But this occasion is already so special. If we made it any more special, we might end up making it less special.
jokes.setup: Marge saving perfume for special occasion
jokes.punchline: Convoluted logic about making occasions too special
jokes.explanation: Marge's overthinking creates circular logic that confuses even herself

jokes.text: It's hard for me to judge since I've never bowled in my life!
jokes.setup: Homer asking if the bowling ball is beautiful
jokes.punchline: Marge's pointed response about never bowling
jokes.explanation: Marge's rare moment of sarcasm, setting up the episode's central conflict

jokes.text: I couldn't very well chop your hand off and bring it to the store.
jokes.setup: Marge pointing out the finger holes are Homer-sized
jokes.punchline: Homer's absurdly literal solution to the problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - acknowledging the problem while completely missing the point

jokes.text: You can't take it back. You had your name engraved on it! - So you'd know it was from me!
jokes.setup: Homer offering to return the obviously self-intended gift
jokes.punchline: His desperate justification for his own name being on it
jokes.explanation: Homer's increasingly desperate attempts to justify the unjustifiable

jokes.text: What? But you don't know how to bowl. Oops.
jokes.setup: Marge declaring she'll keep and use the ball
jokes.punchline: Homer accidentally revealing he never expected her to use it
jokes.explanation: Homer's verbal slip completely exposes his true intentions

jokes.text: I'm here out of spite. - Can't bowl without a lane.
jokes.setup: Worker asking if Marge needs a lane
jokes.punchline: Her honest admission of spiteful motivation
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical - Marge's blunt honesty creates unexpected humor

jokes.text: 13 double 'A'? This is the closest I've got. A nine and a fifteen.
jokes.setup: Marge needing unusually large bowling shoes
jokes.punchline: Being given mismatched sizes that don't even add up right
jokes.explanation: Absurd practical solution to an impossible shoe size request

jokes.text: A little warm and moist. Ugh!
jokes.setup: Marge puts on used bowling shoes
jokes.punchline: Disgusted reaction to gross bowling shoes
jokes.explanation: Standard gross-out humor. Relatable but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: Oh, no, no. Homer's my... ball's name. - I'm Marge.
jokes.setup: Jacques assuming Homer is her husband's name
jokes.punchline: Awkward correction that it's her bowling ball's name
jokes.explanation: Awkward meet-cute complicated by the bowling ball situation

jokes.text: Many people have senseless attachments to heavy, clumsy things, such as this Homer of yours.
jokes.setup: Jacques commenting on attachments to bowling balls
jokes.punchline: Double meaning referring to both ball and husband
jokes.explanation: Brilliant double entendre that works on both literal and metaphorical levels

jokes.text: Pick up Homer. Pick him up. - Now throw. Throw, damn you.
jokes.setup: Jacques instructing Marge on bowling technique
jokes.punchline: Inadvertently telling her to throw her husband
jokes.explanation: Continues the ball/husband double meaning with increasing intensity

jokes.text: But first of all, you yell, 'The eight-pin is a cop.' - Let it out, Marge. Laugh loud. Laugh out loud. You'll lose weight.
jokes.setup: Jacques teaching bowling technique
jokes.punchline: Bizarre bowling superstition combined with weight loss advice
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully random bowling 'wisdom' that reveals Jacques' character

jokes.text: Hot pizza-- the food of kings.
jokes.setup: Homer eating dinner with the kids
jokes.punchline: Elevating pizza to royal status
jokes.explanation: Homer's grandiose description of basic food perfectly captures his character

jokes.text: Does the time always drag like this?
jokes.setup: Homer claiming he wants to spend time with his kids
jokes.punchline: Immediately complaining about how slow time passes
jokes.explanation: Homer's complete lack of parental instincts exposed in one line

jokes.text: Caress it. Experience it. Quite smooth, isn't it, Marge? You could eat off of it. You hungry? Four onion rings!
jokes.setup: Jacques's sensual lane-touching lesson building sexual tension
jokes.punchline: Abrupt shift to ordering greasy bowling alley food
jokes.explanation: Great deflation of the seductive mood. The sensual buildup crashing into 'four onion rings' is perfect comedy timing.

jokes.text: All right! We're clean!
jokes.setup: Homer attempting to clean up after dinner
jokes.punchline: Declaring victory despite obvious disaster
jokes.explanation: Homer's delusional standards for cleanliness create visual comedy

jokes.text: Sport, dear. It's a sport, you silly thing.
jokes.setup: Marge calling bowling a challenging hobby
jokes.punchline: Jacques' condescending correction
jokes.explanation: Reveals Jacques' patronizing nature beneath the charm

jokes.text: It's really for me. Seventeen-fifty. Enjoy it, my darling.
jokes.setup: Jacques giving Marge personalized bowling shoes
jokes.punchline: Immediately charging her for them
jokes.explanation: Undercuts romantic gesture by immediately demanding payment

jokes.text: My mind says,'Stop,' but my heart... and my hips cry,' Proceed.'
jokes.setup: Jacques expressing internal conflict about pursuing Marge
jokes.punchline: Including hips in the emotional decision-making process
jokes.explanation: Absurd addition of body parts to romantic declaration

jokes.text: Not at Barney's Bowlarama. Away from the thunderous folly of clattering pins.
jokes.setup: Jacques wants to see Marge away from bowling
jokes.punchline: Overwrought poetic description of bowling sounds
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jacques pretentiousness. 'Thunderous folly' is wonderfully overwrought for bowling.

jokes.text: It's not quite breakfast, it's not quite lunch, but it comes with a slice of cantaloupee at the end. You don't get completely what you would at breakfast, but you get a good meal.
jokes.setup: Marge asking what brunch is
jokes.punchline: Overly detailed explanation that makes brunch sound terrible
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deconstruction of brunch that makes it sound like a scam

jokes.text: There are ten pins in my heart. You've knocked over eight. Won't you please pick up that spare?
jokes.setup: Jacques making romantic appeal
jokes.punchline: Extended bowling metaphor for love
jokes.explanation: Perfect extended metaphor that's both romantic and ridiculous

jokes.text: Oh, Bart, don't you see? This is what psychologists call overcompensation. Mom is wracked with guilt because her marriage is failing.
jokes.setup: Bart enjoying the elaborate lunches
jokes.punchline: Lisa's clinical analysis of their mother's behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - adult psychological insight from an 8-year-old

jokes.text: You're in stage two, denial. - No, I'm not. - Yes, you are. - I'm not. I'm not. Am not! - I stand corrected.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining stages of grief to Bart
jokes.punchline: Bart demonstrating denial while denying he's in denial
jokes.explanation: Perfect demonstration of the concept while the character denies it

jokes.text: I'm a married women. Don't call me that. - No, no, no, no, no. Mimosa is the name of the drink.
jokes.setup: Jacques offering Marge a mimosa
jokes.punchline: Marge thinking it's a sexual term
jokes.explanation: Marge's innocence creates misunderstanding about innocent drink name

jokes.text: Let's hope something runs over her.
jokes.setup: Helen Lovejoy leaving after gossiping
jokes.punchline: Jacques' casually murderous wish
jokes.explanation: Shockingly casual violence delivered with perfect timing

jokes.text: You're so naive. They are for lovemaking. - Really? - Yes.
jokes.setup: Marge commenting on Jacques' bowling trophies
jokes.punchline: Jacques claiming they're for sexual prowess
jokes.explanation: Absurd seduction technique that reveals Jacques' ridiculousness

jokes.text: Some divine pinspotter must have placed us side by side. Like two fragile bowling pins-- Standing bravely-- Until inevitably-- We must topple.
jokes.setup: Jacques waxing poetic about their meeting
jokes.punchline: Extended bowling metaphor for romantic fate
jokes.explanation: Continues Jacques' pattern of overwrought bowling metaphors

jokes.text: Dad, you didn't even say 'ouch.' - Oh, sorry. Ouch.
jokes.setup: Baseball hits Homer in the face
jokes.punchline: Homer's delayed, obligatory response to pain
jokes.explanation: Homer's depression so deep he forgets to react to physical pain

jokes.text: You told me when something's bothering you and you're too damn stupid to know what to do, just keep your fool mouth shut. At least that way, you won't make things worse. - Hmm. Good advice.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to help Homer with his own advice
jokes.punchline: Homer's self-deprecating wisdom about his own stupidity
jokes.explanation: Homer's rare moment of self-awareness disguised as wisdom

jokes.text: I don't know how you do it. Youjust got a gift, I guess. I've always thought so. I just never mentioned it, but it's time you knew how I feel.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to be romantic about sandwich-making
jokes.punchline: Awkward, desperate attempt at appreciation
jokes.explanation: Homer's clumsy attempt at romance through mundane domestic skills

jokes.text: How can I eat it? She made it. It's all I have left.
jokes.setup: Barney asking why Homer won't eat his sandwich
jokes.punchline: Homer treating sandwich as sacred relic of marriage
jokes.explanation: Pathetic but touching moment of Homer's attachment to Marge

jokes.text: Tell him I'm going to the backseat of my car with the woman I love, and I won't be back for ten minutes.
jokes.setup: Mr. Burns asking what to tell the boss
jokes.punchline: Homer's oversharing about romantic reconciliation timeline
jokes.explanation: Homer's inappropriate honesty and oddly specific time estimate
